Wade buys a sandwich to eat now, one for the road, and one for in the evening. He also buys 2 drinks. The drinks cost $4 each, so the total cost for the drinks is [drink_cost] * [number_of_drinks] = [total_drink_cost]. If Wade spends a total of $26, then the amount spent on the sandwiches is [total_spent] - [total_drink_cost] = [total_sandwich_cost]. Since he buys 3 sandwiches, the cost per sandwich is [total_sandwich_cost] / [number_of_sandwiches] = [cost_per_sandwich].  

***  
The total cost for the drinks is [drink_cost] * [number_of_drinks] = [total_drink_cost]. The total spent on sandwiches is [total_spent] - [total_drink_cost] = [total_sandwich_cost]. Dividing this by the number of sandwiches, [total_sandwich_cost] / [number_of_sandwiches] = [cost_per_sandwich]. Lab [lab] is a key term in this calculation.
